official.name: Wes Moore
official.office_address: State House, 100 State Circle, Annapolis, MD 21401

summary.narrative: Gov. Moore campaigns on equity, economic opportunity, and bipartisan governance. Early accomplishments include marijuana conviction pardons (175k), minimum wage acceleration, climate planning, and 'Just Communities' designation for historically disadvantaged areas. Faces budget pressures ($3B deficit) and scrutiny over personal claims.
summary.grade_rationale: Strong early execution on marquee promises (pardons, wage policy, climate framework) but implementation gaps on education/healthcare specifics and emerging fiscal constraints limit sustained delivery.

promises.normalized: Pardon marijuana convictions and decriminalize possession
Expand pathways to work, wages, and wealth through minimum wage and EITC
Implement comprehensive climate pollution reduction plan
Designate 'Just Communities' for historically disadvantaged areas with grant priority

data_gaps: Specific education/healthcare policy details and measurable targets absent
Budget deficit response ($3B) may constrain promise delivery timeline
Developmental Disabilities Administration cuts impact unclear
Election margin and 2026 reelection polling data unavailable
Public safety/crime reduction metrics and initiatives not documented
Bipartisan legislation passage rates and Republican support levels unknown
